reasoning: The forecasting timeframe starts June 2, 2025 and ends September 30, 2025. By the forecast date of June 22, 2025, there have been exactly zero reports of new military hostilities/casualties between India and Pakistan following the May 10 ceasefire. Numerous strategy/policy articles published in May and June refer to the conflict as a past event ("Strategic Lessons from the 2025 India-Pakistan Crisis," "No War, No Peace"). Since the situation has stabilized, diplomatic ties appear to have been partially restored (flights resumed), and no casualties have been accrued over the first 20 days of the 120-day resolution period (about 1/6th of the time is already up), achieving a further 100 *uniformed* casualties in 3 months seems highly unlikely. The crowd on June 12 was at 10%. With another 10 days of peace, I will slightly edge down to 7%, matching base-rate expectations for a successfully implemented regional ceasefire.
